narration: Here's something nobody's telling you — your groceries are shrinking. Companies call it shrinkflation, and it's quietly draining $2,900 a year from your wallet.
visual: Shopping cart SVG with two stats animating simultaneously: price ticking UP ($7.99 to $11.49) and package size ticking DOWN (64oz to 52oz). Bold 'SHRINKFLATION' headline drops in from above.

narration: Companies keep the price the same but quietly shrink the package. Your orange juice went from 64 ounces to 52 — same price, way less juice.
visual: Two orange juice cartons side by side — 2020 version taller labeled 64 OZ at $4.99 in green, vs 2024 version shorter labeled 52 OZ at $4.99 in red. Arrow between them. Light background.

narration: Since 2020, products shrank 15% while prices jumped 26%. That combo hits you with a 49% effective price hike — and most people never even noticed.
visual: Three SVG bars growing from baseline: red bar for -15% package size, amber bar for +26% price, green bar for 49% effective hike (tallest). Each label springs in as bar grows. Dark background.

narration: Number one, orange juice: 64 ounces down to 52. Number two, chips: 16 ounces down to 13. Number three, ice cream: 56 ounces to 48. Same. Exact. Price.
visual: Three product cards sliding in from the left one by one — each showing product name, before oz in green, arrow, after oz in red. Numbered 1, 2, 3 in amber. Light background.

narration: Average household drops $9,500 on groceries every year. With a 49% effective hike, that's $2,900 in extra spending — for smaller packages than you bought five years ago.
visual: Dark background with the equation animating in: $9,500/year label, then x49% effective hike, then counter spinning up to $2,900 in red. 'PER YEAR' label underneath.

narration: Just check price per ounce — not per package. That one habit puts $2,900 back in your pocket every year. Follow for more hidden money traps.
visual: Price tag SVG showing $0.078/oz in green on light background, animated green checkmark, '$2,900 SAVED A YEAR' in red fades in. CTA 'FOLLOW FOR MORE MONEY TRAPS' appears last.
